crime.value: Murder by carriage - Christopher Helmsley killed the Duke's daughter by running her over with her own carriage
crime.quote: Christopher Helmsley took the daughter of his employer, and ran her over in her own carriage.

victim.value: The Duke of Norfolk's daughter - made vulnerable as the daughter of Christopher's employer, creating opportunity and access
victim.quote: Christopher Helmsley took the daughter of his employer, and ran her over in her own carriage.

culprit_and_motive.value: Christopher Helmsley killed his employer's daughter, though specific motive is not detailed in the text
culprit_and_motive.quote: Christopher Helmsley took the daughter of his employer, and ran her over in her own carriage. That really is depravity.

resolution.value: Holmes solved the case by identifying the body in the mortuary as the missing Duke's daughter and determining Christopher was the killer
resolution.quote: I found their missing daughter... The body in the mortuary was the missing Duke's daughter

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes as the primary detective, with Dr. Watson as narrator and assistant
investigator.quote: In all the years I had known Sherlock Holmes

suspect_architecture.value: Single deceased perpetrator (Christopher Helmsley) revealed posthumously - no living suspects to interrogate or differentiate
suspect_architecture.quote: Christopher Helmsley took the daughter of his employer, and ran her over in her own carriage.

red_herring.value: Holmes's apparent drunken incapacitation and disinterest masks his active investigation and disguised fieldwork
red_herring.quote: Pretending to be drunk meant you would never expect me to leave.

clue_fairness.value: Missing newspaper headline about Duke's daughter shown to reader when Watson reads it aloud to Holmes
clue_fairness.quote: Daughter of Duke missing, Detectives baffled.

social_world.value: Aristocratic hierarchy - Duke/Duchess of Norfolk as employers of Christopher, with power to reward Holmes handsomely
social_world.quote: The Duke and Duchess of Norfolk

reveal_mechanic.value: Body identification at mortuary connects missing Duke's daughter to Richard's wife, revealing the perpetrator's identity
reveal_mechanic.quote: The body in the mortuary was the missing Duke's daughter in the paper five months ago

media_and_audience.value: Newspaper coverage of missing Duke's daughter initially dismissed by Holmes, later proves central to case resolution
media_and_audience.quote: Daughter of Duke missing, Detectives baffled.

investigator_wound.value: Holmes's addiction and psychological deterioration, exacerbated by memories of Irene Adler, affecting his investigative behavior
investigator_wound.quote: I noticed the picture of Irene Adler, sitting pride of place on the table.

_meta.title: ebook marion smith the adventure of the posthumous apparition
